CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-30-2014 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-10-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-15-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jan-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances: Richard H. Schoenberger of Walkup Melodia Kelly & Schoenberger (415.981.7210) for plaintiff and responding party; David W. Ammons of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.3954) for defendant and moving party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. (CCP 437c(f)(1).) Defendant CCSF moves for summary adjudication of parts of a cause of action that will not dispose of the entire negligence cause of action. See Notice of Motion, p. 1:27 2:1; Complaint, 10(a), (e) and (f).) This is not permitted by statute that is the basis for the motion. See CCP 437c(f)(1) ("A motion for summary adjudication shall be granted only if it completely disposes of a cause of action . '"). The allegations pertaining to entrustment, training and supervision are not separate and distinct wrongful acts giving rise to separate causes of action. The authority cited by Defendant is distinguishable. Edward Fineman Co. v. Superior Court (1998) 66 Cal. App. 4th 1110, 1118 involved summary adjudication that claims as to 23 out of 83 checks were time barred, because each was a "separate and distinct wrongful act" and because "notwithstanding the aggregation of the 23 1991-1993 checks, each constitutes a separate cause of action." Similarly, Lilienthal & Fowler v. Sup.Ct. (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1848, 1851, 1854, cited by Defendant in reply, permitted summary adjudication in a legal malpractice action where plaintiffs "sought to recover damages based on two separate and distinct obligations" combined in the same cause of action that had "no relation to each other and involve legal services performed at different times, with different and distinct obligations and distinct and separate alleged damages." Plaintiffs here have multiple legal theories, but they pertain to a single primary right. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185 (302/MJM)
